info.name: Benchmark Server API
info.description:
API collection for the LLM Benchmark Server - run, control, and manage benchmark tests.

## Quick Start
1. Hit 'Health Check' to confirm server is up
2. Use one of the 'Trigger Benchmark' requests — run_id is auto-saved to {{run_id}}
3. Poll 'Get Run Status' to track progress
4. Fetch 'Download Report' when state = completed

## Infrastructure (AWS / Azure RCA tests)
RCA queries test against live broken cloud infrastructure.
Use deploy_infra + skip_nuke flags to control the lifecycle:
  - deploy_infra: true  → deploy bootstrap + scenarios before tests
  - skip_nuke: false    → destroy infrastructure after tests
  - skip_nuke: true     → keep infrastructure up (for re-runs / debugging)

## Index Reference (benchmark_aws.json)
  - 0–303   → command queries (no infra needed)
  - 304–333 → RCA queries (infra required, one scenario per index)
    304=E01, 305=E02, ... 313=E10
    314=M01, 315=M02, ... 323=M10
    324=H01, 325=H02, ... 333=H10

## Index Reference (benchmark_azure.json)
  - 0–19  → command queries
  - 20–39 → RCA queries
    20=E01, 21=E02, ... 29=E10
    30=M01, 31=M02, ... 39=M10

variable.description: Auto-populated from the last Trigger Benchmark response

request.description:
Verify the benchmark server is running.
Expected response: {"status": "ok"}

exec: // Auto-save run_id so other requests can use {{run_id}}

request.description:
Run a single AWS command query (index 0).
Command queries (indices 0-303) do NOT need cloud infrastructure.
deploy_infra is false — no CloudFormation stacks are created.

exec: // Auto-save run_id so other requests can use {{run_id}}

request.description:
Full lifecycle for a single AWS RCA scenario:
  1. Deploy bootstrap (shared VPC + S3) + E01 CloudFormation stack (Broken mode)
  2. Run the RCA benchmark query against live broken infrastructure
  3. Nuke all stacks after completion

Note: CloudFormation deploy takes ~5-10 min before the test starts.
Check status via GET /agent-benchmark/{{run_id}}/status

item.name: AWS - RCA Single Scenario (deploy, keep infra)

request.description:
Deploy infra, run test, but KEEP infrastructure up.
Useful for:
  - Debugging a failed test (infra stays up for manual inspection)
  - Running the same test multiple times without redeploying

Follow up with 'AWS - RCA Re-run (infra already up)' for subsequent runs.
Manually nuke when done: bash llm/agents/aws/aws-agent-test/scripts/nuke.sh

Re-run a test against already-deployed infrastructure.
Use after 'AWS - RCA Single Scenario (deploy, keep infra)'.

No deploy step → test starts immediately.

Full benchmark run: all 334 AWS queries including all 30 RCA scenarios.

Lifecycle:
  1. Deploy bootstrap (shared VPC + S3 bucket)
  2. Deploy all 30 scenario stacks in Broken mode (~30-40 min)
  3. Run all 334 benchmark queries
  4. Nuke all stacks
  5. Email report to user + cc_emails

Use status endpoint to monitor progress.

Full lifecycle for a single Azure RCA scenario:
  1. Deploy bootstrap (shared App Service Plan)
  2. Deploy E01 ARM template in Broken mode
  3. Run the RCA benchmark query
  4. Delete resource groups (async)

Azure ARM deploys are faster than CloudFormation (~2-5 min per scenario).

Run a single Azure command query (no infrastructure needed).
Command queries (indices 0-19) just call the Azure agent with az CLI questions.

Pause a running benchmark.
The current query will complete before pausing — it won't cut mid-query.
Resume with the Resume endpoint.
Resume a paused benchmark from where it left off.

Stop a running benchmark permanently.
A partial report is generated with the queries completed so far.
Cannot be resumed after stopping.

Poll this to track progress.

Response fields:
  - state: running | paused | stopped | completed
  - progress: "3/30" (current/total queries)
  - current_query: the query currently being processed

Note: During infra deployment (deploy_infra: true), state stays 'running'
until all CloudFormation/ARM stacks are up — then queries start.

List all tracked benchmark runs with their state, agent, and progress.
Useful to find run_ids from previous sessions.

Download the JSON report for a completed run.

Report contains:
  - details[]: per-query scores (answer_similarity, answer_relevancy, rca_quality_score)
  - summary: overall accuracy, avg RCA score, failed indices

Remove state files for all completed and stopped runs.
Does NOT delete cloud infrastructure — only cleans up local run tracking files.

Remove state file for a specific benchmark run.
Does NOT delete cloud infrastructure.
